all_citations.claim: The organization operates Brighter Horizons Academy and provides scholarships and financial aid to its graduating classes.
The organization's profile includes financial metadata and serves the Muslim community.
The Islamic Services Foundation is zakat-eligible and accepts zakat donations.
The organization reported total revenue of $12,484,881 and maintains a 99.5% program expense ratio.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and long-standing community presence, balanced by a high cost per beneficiary and domestic focus.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary relative to other domestic poverty and education programs.
Limited external data regarding long-term impact metrics beyond graduation rates.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The foundation shows moderate alignment as a zakat-eligible institution serving domestic needs [5]. While it has a proven track record since 1989, the alignment score is tempered by a lower donor fit for those seeking urgent international humanitarian relief, as the mission is primarily focused on domestic educational infrastructure [1][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: ISF demonstrates high financial health with a total revenue of $12,484,881 and a 99.5% program expense ratio [4][6]. While it successfully manages large-scale educational programs and provides full-ride scholarships, the impact score reflects a high cost per beneficiary of approximately $13,220 and a focus on capacity building rather than direct emergency relief [1][6].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Services Foundation is a dedicated non-profit organization providing comprehensive educational services and community support to the Muslim community since 1989.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 99.5%, ensuring nearly all funds directly support community services.
Strong liquidity with 14.5 months of working capital available for operations.
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised.
baseline_narrative.summary: founded in 1989, the Islamic Services Foundation (ISF) operates major educational institutions including Brighter Horizons Academy and Little Horizons Academy [1]. The organization is zakat-eligible and maintains a strong focus on academic excellence and financial aid for its students [5][6].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term Islamic institutional building and want to support established educational infrastructure with high financial efficiency.
donor_motivations: Want to support full-time Islamic schooling in the US
Value organizations with a 30+ year track record
Prefer charities that directs 99.5% to programs
Seek to fulfill zakat through educational scholarships
giving_considerations: The organization is currently navigating a period of revenue contraction
High per-beneficiary costs reflect the intensive nature of private schooling
Governance is centralized with limited independent board oversight
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth startups or those who prioritize organizations with majority-independent boards.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified output tracking (graduates, aid distributed) without independent longitudinal studies.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ISF creates impact by providing high-quality Islamic and academic education to produce community leaders, while offering financial aid to ensure accessibility for needy families.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking graduation rates and aid distribution ensures that the foundation is meeting its mission of community service and educational excellence.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the organization has strong reserves, the <cite id="6">recent 12% revenue decline</cite> suggests a need for increased donor support to maintain current program levels.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="4">100.0% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="4">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
ISF maintains <cite id="4">14.5 months of working capital</cite>, providing a strong safety net for its educational institutions.
The foundation operates with extreme efficiency, spending <cite id="4">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable funds.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Services Foundation demonstrates a high level of fiscal discipline, particularly in its fundraising and program allocation. By maintaining a <cite id="4">99.5% program expense ratio</cite>, the organization ensures that donor contributions are not diluted by administrative overhead. This efficiency is paired with a long-standing <cite id="1">track record dating back to 1989</cite>, establishing it as a mature institution within the American Muslim nonprofit landscape. Furthermore, the organization has successfully diversified its income, with <cite id="6">over $10 million generated through program services</cite>, which stabilizes the foundation against fluctuations in private donations.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1989, the Islamic Services Foundation (ISF) has served the Muslim community for over three decades through a diverse portfolio of educational and humanitarian programs. The organization operates several key institutions, including <cite id="1">Brighter Horizons Academy and Little Horizons Academy</cite>, which provide academic and religious instruction. Financial data from 2023 shows the organization manages <cite id="6">$14,235,728 in total revenue</cite>, with a significant portion derived from <cite id="6">$10,415,037 in program service revenue</cite>. This indicates a sustainable model where service fees complement charitable contributions. While the organization maintains <cite id="4">a program expense ratio of 99.5%</cite>, it recently experienced a <cite id="6">12% decline in revenue</cite> from the previous fiscal year. Beyond its core schools, ISF manages the <cite id="1">ISF Qur’an Institute and various Sunday school programs</cite>. The foundation also facilitates humanitarian efforts, reporting that it distributed <cite id="1">$1,000,000 in financial aid</cite> to support student access. Their impact extends to health services, having <cite id="1">treated 1,074 children via medical missions</cite> according to recent reporting. As a <cite id="5">zakat-eligible organization</cite>, ISF provides a structured pathway for donors to fulfill religious obligations through education and poverty relief.
